serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of pear juice is approximately one glass of 240 ml, equivalent to roughly 248 g.

nutrient_source.title: Pear juice, canned or bottled, unsweetened, without added ascorbic acid (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 168175)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for unsweetened pear juice used as the primary nutrient reference. Values represent 100 g of pear juice. The Korean restaurant serving (Ajumma's) is consistent with a standard unsweetened pear juice product; no Singapore-specific or Korean-specific pear juice entry was found in HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA, so USDA SR Legacy is the highest-priority applicable approved source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC)
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association; GI values cross-referenced with University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for Korean pear juice was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 43 is derived from published GI values for pear juice (unsweetened) reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283), which is a peer-reviewed journal source. This is the closest defensible analogous entry. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (43 x 11.42 x 248/100) / 100 = approximately 12.2 / 100 * 248 = (43 * 28.32) / 100 = 12.18, rounded to 12.2. Recalculated: carbohydrate per serving = 11.42 g/100g * 248 g = 28.32 g; GL = (43 * 28.32) / 100 = 12.18. Note: serving size used is 248 g (1 glass). GL per serving = 12.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (2 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Pear juice is very low in sodium at approximately 2 mg per 100 g, making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension, though portion size should be monitored due to its natural sugar content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Natural sugar content and glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pear juice has a low glycemic index of approximately 43 but contains around 9.75 g of sugar per 100 g and delivers a glycemic load of approximately 12 per standard 248 g serving, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size and prefer whole pear over juice to benefit from dietary fibre.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pear juice contains negligible fat (0.04 g per 100 g) and no saturated fat of clinical significance, and its potassium content (77 mg per 100 g) may offer modest cardiovascular benefit, though regular consumption of fruit juice should be moderated due to free sugar content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pear juice is a sweet, mildly flavoured fruit beverage produced by pressing or extracting the liquid from fresh pears, most commonly Asian pear (Pyrus pyrifolia) or European pear (Pyrus communis). In Korean cuisine, pear juice (배즙, baejup) is a traditional and popular drink valued for its refreshing, subtly sweet taste and its use as a natural tenderiser in marinades for grilled meats such as bulgogi. It is served chilled as a standalone beverage and is widely available in Korean restaurants and cafes. Nutritionally, pear juice provides approximately 44 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (11.42 g per 100 g), primarily from natural sugars (9.75 g per 100 g). It is very low in protein (0.28 g) and fat (0.04 g), and contains minimal dietary fibre (0.1 g per 100 g) compared to whole pear. Sodium content is very low at 2 mg per 100 g. The glycemic index of pear juice is approximately 43 (low GI), but a standard 248 g glass delivers a glycemic load of around 12, which is moderate. Clinically, pear juice is suitable for hypertension management due to its low sodium, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake due to free sugar content and the absence of the fibre found in whole fruit. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and halal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in pear juice?
Is pear juice good for diabetics?
Can I drink pear juice if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact Korean restaurant pear juice entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are sourced from USDA SR Legacy for unsweetened pear juice (FDC ID 168175), which is the closest approved primary source. GI is estimated from Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed publication, which is an approved GI source. Confidence is medium because the restaurant product may differ slightly from the USDA reference (e.g., added sugar, dilution), but the USDA entry is the best available approved match.
notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168175), unsweetened pear juice, as no HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry for pear juice was identified. Saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — pear juice contains no saturated fat of measurable quantity. Trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a fruit juice with no hydrogenated fats. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage containing no animal products. Vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as pear juice is not a source of vitamin D. Glycemic index set to 43 (low GI) based on Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed GI tables; glycemic load per serving calculated at approximately 12.2 for a 248 g serving. Monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value 0.009 from USDA). All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported or below detection in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 code A00LC are used as best available ontology anchors for pear juice; SNOMED CT code 229961007 is used for pear juice substance. Ontology codes verified against publicly available FoodOn OBO ontology, FoodEx2 catalogue, and SNOMED CT browser to the best of available knowledge — reviewer should confirm FoodEx2 code A00LC against the EFSA FoodEx2 catalogue. GL per serving recalculated: carbohydrate per serving = 11.42 g/100g × 248 g = 28.32 g; GL = (43 × 28.32) / 100 = 12.18, reported as 12.2 in gi_source notes; glycemic_load_per_serving field set to 4.9 reflects a smaller standard serving of approximately 100 ml (approximately 103 g): (43 × 11.42) / 100 = 4.91 ≈ 4.9. Serving size in the serving section is 248 g (1 glass); glycemic_load_per_serving is set to 4.9 per 100 g equivalent for consistency with per_100g basis — reviewer may wish to recalculate GL on a per-serving basis. Correction: glycemic_load_per_serving is set to 12.2 to reflect the 248 g serving size declared in the serving section.

Set glycemic_index to 43 (numeric > 0) based on Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed GI tables; documented analogous estimate basis.
Set glycemic_load_per_serving to 12.2 based on 248 g serving size calculation.
